headline: P&G warns of $1 billion profit hit from higher oil prices
summary: STORY: :: Procter & GambleProcter & Gamble on Friday warned of a roughly $1 billion hit to its profit over its next fiscal year due to surging oil prices.That's among the highest profit losses for any company due to the Iran war outside of airlines, which rely heavily on oil for fuel.:: Procter & GambleP&G's estimated profit hit, projected for its fiscal year starting in July, accounts for oil prices jumping from $60 a barrel before the Middle East conflict to around $100 today.The consumer goods giant said that will impact plastics and paper for packaging, as well as transportation charges.While P&G on a post-earnings call said it was well-placed to manage the challenges, the company's finance chief noted that, quote, "a billion dollars after tax is nothing to sneeze at from a headwind standpoint."The company is the latest to join a host of global firms flagging significant cost pressures from the Iran war.European rival Nestle has warned of higher costs due to the Strait of Hormuz blockade, while Beiersdorf, maker of Nivea, is considering price hikes later this year if commodity costs continue to rise.A Reuters review of statements from 172 companies since the start of the Iran war showed 24 of them have either withdrawn or cut their outlook, while 35 have signaled price hikes and another 35 companies have warned of a financial hit from the conflict.:: Procter & GambleAs for P&G's quarterly results, the company did beat sales and earnings estimates, sending its shares up as much as 5% in Friday trading.
